name: Texas Exchange Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank operating since 1970 with locations in Crowley and Fort Worth, Texas, offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and online banking services.
description_long:
Texas Exchange Bank was established in 1970 and opened its flagship location in Crowley, Texas in 1974. The bank expanded to Fort Worth in November 2010, maintaining its commitment to community-focused banking. As an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, the bank positions itself as a stable, federally-protected financial institution.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking products. Personal customers can access checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), along with personal loans. Business customers receive similar deposit products plus dedicated business loan options. All customers can access online banking with features including account inquiries, funds transfers, bill payment, loan payments, stop payments, check image viewing, and downloadable account data compatible with QuickBooks, Quicken, and Microsoft Money.

Texas Exchange Bank differentiates itself through its emphasis on personalized, community-oriented service. The bank explicitly highlights that it is "the only Crowley-based bank that has stayed committed to the community" and positions its experienced bankers as "seasoned professionals who understand all of their needs, but still have the local touch." The bank's employees are noted as active in social and civic activities. The bank also offers additional services including voice response banking, wire transfers, debit cards, ATM cards, and safe deposit boxes.

The bank serves traditional banking needs for individuals and small businesses seeking FDIC protection and personal service in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. However, prospective customers should note that online information is limited—specific interest rates, fee schedules, and loan terms are not detailed on the accessible website content, and the bank has only two physical locations in Texas.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
53+ years of banking history (established 1970) demonstrating stability
Comprehensive personal and business banking products (checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, loans)
Online banking with bill payment, funds transfers, check imaging, and QuickBooks/Quicken integration
Local community focus with employees active in civic engagement in Crowley and Fort Worth
Voice response banking, wire transfers, debit cards, ATM cards, and safe deposit boxes available
Equal opportunity employer with commitment to accessibility and reasonable accommodations
cons: Limited geographic footprint: only two locations in Texas (Crowley and Fort Worth)
Website does not display current interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparison
No apparent mobile app mentioned despite offering online banking services
Limited information about loan products, terms, rates, or application processes
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or credit card products
best_for: Crowley, Texas and Fort Worth residents seeking personalized, community-focused banking
Small business owners needing dedicated business checking, savings, and loan products
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and relationship banking over digital-first services
Individuals seeking basic banking services (checking, savings, CDs) with local branch access
diagnosis: Texas Exchange Bank is best for Crowley and Fort Worth area residents and small business owners who value personalized community banking and FDIC protection over digital convenience. The main caveat is the bank's limited geographic reach with only two Texas locations, and lack of transparent rate and fee information online, making it unsuitable for those seeking wide branch networks or those outside the Dallas-Fort Worth area.
services: Online banking with bill payment and funds transfers
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
